# FilesHub — full LLM context

> FilesHub (https://fileshub.zaions.com) is a zero-cost file-storage, email, and
> developer-utility API built on Laravel + Nova. This file gives an AI assistant enough
> detail to answer integration questions accurately without crawling every page. A
> machine-readable OpenAPI 3.1 spec is at https://fileshub-docs.zaions.com/openapi.json, and
> every doc page is mirrored as raw Markdown under /raw/ (index /raw/manifest.json).
> Last updated: 2026-07-14.

## What FilesHub is

A small HTTP API that stores files for you and serves them back at a stable URL, sends
transactional email from verified domains, and exposes a large catalogue of stateless
developer utilities. Apps use it as their upload + email backend so they never have to run
their own. Every request is authenticated with a per-project API key sent in the `X-API-Key`
header. The API is versioned under `/api/v1` (two status endpoints, `/health` and `/version`,
live at `/api`).

Base URL: `https://fileshub.zaions.com/api/v1`

## Authentication

Send `X-API-Key: <key>` on every request. Keys belong to a project and carry scopes:

- `read` — list and download (including private) objects; list jobs, templates, schedules.
- `write` — upload and delete objects; manage templates and schedules.
- `email` — send email.
- `email_template` — manage email templates (with `write`).
- per-utility service scopes (e.g. `converter`, `qr_code`) — all enabled by default.

Keys may be `live` (`fh_live_...`) or `test` (`fh_test_...`). A **restricted** key only works
from allowlisted origins so you can ship it in a frontend with no proxy backend:

- Web — the browser's `Origin`/`Referer` is checked against allowed domains (supports
  `*.example.com` wildcards).
- Android — send `X-App-Id` (or `X-Android-Package`) with the package name; optionally pin
  signing certificates and send a matching SHA-256/SHA-1 in `X-Android-Cert`.
- iOS — send `X-App-Id` with the bundle id.

These are self-declared headers (Google-Maps-key trust model): they stop key reuse from other
apps/sites but are not cryptographic attestation, so keep high-privilege keys server-side.
Create and manage keys and their origins in the Nova admin panel.

## The object lifecycle (storage API)

### Upload — POST /api/v1/objects (write)

Multipart `form-data`:

- `file` (required) — the file. Validated against the configured max size
  (`FILESHUB_MAX_UPLOAD_MB`, default 10 MB) and an optional allowed-MIME list.
- `visibility` (optional) — `public` or `private`. Defaults to `private` server-side, but
  the Aoneahsan/Zaions integration convention is to send `public`.
- `expires_in_days` (optional, 1–3650) — auto-delete N days from now.
- `expires_at` (optional, ISO-8601, must be in the future) — auto-delete at an absolute time.
  If both are given, `expires_at` wins.

Success `201`:

```json
{
  "public_id": "01JDKQXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXX",
  "project_id": "01JDKPXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXX",
  "visibility": "public",
  "mime_type": "application/pdf",
  "size_bytes": 1048576,
  "url": "https://fileshub.zaions.com/api/v1/objects/01JDKQXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXX",
  "expires_at": null
}
```

The `url` is what you store in your own database and render in your UI. The `public_id` is a
ULID — sortable, URL-safe, collision-resistant.

### Download / view — GET /api/v1/objects/{public_id}

Streams the file with its real `Content-Type` and original filename. Public objects need no
key (use the URL directly in an `<img>` or `<a>`); private objects require a `read` key from
the same project. Expired objects return `404` and are cleaned up on access.

### List — GET /api/v1/objects (read)

Query params: `page` (default 1), `per_page` (default 20, max 100), `visibility`
(`public`|`private`, optional filter). Returns:

```json
{
  "data": [
    {
      "public_id": "01JDKQ...",
      "original_filename": "photo.jpg",
      "mime_type": "image/jpeg",
      "size_bytes": 524288,
      "visibility": "public",
      "url": "https://fileshub.zaions.com/api/v1/objects/01JDKQ...",
      "created_at": "2026-06-23T12:00:00+00:00"
    }
  ],
  "meta": { "current_page": 1, "per_page": 20, "total": 150 }
}
```

### Delete — DELETE /api/v1/objects/{public_id} (write)

Permanently deletes the database record and the stored file (a model event removes the file).
Returns `200 { "message": "Deleted" }`. Only objects belonging to the key's project are
deletable.

## Error envelope

Errors return a JSON body `{ "message": "..." }` with an appropriate status code:

- `401` — missing/invalid key, or a private object requested without a key.
- `403` — key lacks the required permission, or origin/app-id not allowed.
- `404` — object not found, expired, or the stored file is missing.
- `422` — validation failed (e.g. file too large, bad `visibility`). The message is the first
  validation error.
- `429` — rate limit exceeded.
- `500` — server error.

## Email API

- Send — `POST /api/v1/emails/send` (email scope). Raw (`subject` + `body_html`/`body_text`) or
  `template` + `variables`. One `to` per call; optional `to_name`, `cc`/`bcc` (<=10), `reply_to`,
  `from_name`, `attachments` (FilesHub object ULIDs, <=5), `domain` (`aoneahsan.com` default,
  `zaions.com`, `trizlink.com`), `queue` (default true => `202 Accepted` with `job_id` +
  `status_url`; false => synchronous `201`), `scheduled_at` (one future send). Errors: `422`
  validation / unknown domain, `429` quota, `503` domain temporarily without capacity.
- Templates — `GET /api/v1/emails/templates` (read); `POST`/`PATCH`/`DELETE
  /api/v1/emails/templates{/slug}` (write + email_template). Fields: `name`, `slug` (immutable),
  `category`, `subject`, `body_html`/`body_text`, `variables`. Send with `template` + `variables`.
- Schedules — `GET`/`POST`/`PATCH`/`DELETE /api/v1/email-schedules{/id}` + `POST
  /api/v1/email-schedules/{id}/run`. `cron_expression` (5-field or `@hourly`/`@daily`/`@weekly`/
  `@monthly`), `timezone` (IANA), `recipients` (1–50, one email each per run), template or raw
  content. `PATCH {"is_active": false}` pauses.

## Jobs & status

- Jobs — `GET /api/v1/jobs` (read; paginated, default 20 / max 50; filters `status`, `type`) and
  `GET /api/v1/jobs/{job_id}`. A job serializes `{ id, type, status, attempts, result, error,
  queued_at, started_at, finished_at }`; status is `pending` -> `processing` -> `completed` /
  `failed`. Queued email retries 3x with backoff.
- Health — `GET /api/health` (no key): `{ status, app, version, backend_version, database,
  timestamp }`; `status` is `ok` or `degraded`.
- Version — `GET /api/version` (no key): `{ backend_version, env }` deploy marker (code constant,
  `YYYY.MM.DD.N`).

## Management API (control plane)

Alongside the data plane, FilesHub has a server-side **Management API** at
`https://fileshub.zaions.com/api/public/v1` for administering the account itself: projects, API keys,
and origin restrictions. It is authenticated by a **user-level access token** (prefix `fh_pat_`) sent
as `Authorization: Bearer fh_pat_...` (or `X-Access-Token`). This is a different token family from the
per-project `fh_live_` API keys — an `fh_live_` key is rejected here (`INVALID_ACCESS_TOKEN_FORMAT`)
and an `fh_pat_` token does nothing on `/api/v1`. The plane sends no CORS headers and is server/CLI
only; never put an access token in a browser or a committed file.

A token covers all projects (existing and future — the default) or an explicit subset; a resource the
token does not cover returns `404 NOT_FOUND`, never 403, so scopes cannot be enumerated. A separate,
off-by-default boolean `can_manage_supabase` gates the Supabase project vault (below) — a distinct axis
from the project scope, and `GET /token` reports it. Success is `{ "data": ... }`; errors are
`{ "error": { "code", "message", "details? } }`. The plane is rate limited to 120 requests/minute.
Create tokens in the FilesHub admin under Access Tokens.

Endpoints:
- `GET /token` — introspect the token (name, scope, expiry). The intended first call.
- Projects — `GET /projects` (`?q=` name/slug search, `?status=`, paginated), `POST /projects`,
  and `GET|PATCH|DELETE /projects/{project}` where `{project}` is an id, ULID public_id, or slug.
  Delete cascades the project's keys, origins, stored objects, and audit logs.
- API keys — `GET|POST /projects/{project}/api-keys`, `GET|PATCH|DELETE /projects/{project}/api-keys/{id}`,
  `POST .../{id}/rotate` (new secret, old dies), `POST .../{id}/reveal` (re-read the stored plaintext).
  Create and rotate return `plaintext_key` once; the list/detail representation never includes the
  hash or plaintext.
- Origins — `GET|POST /projects/{project}/api-keys/{id}/origins` and `PATCH|DELETE .../origins/{id}`.
  Domain values are validated against the same scheme+port policy as the dashboard and stored
  canonically (`example.com` -> `https://example.com`); `http://` on a public host is a 422, a repeat
  is a 409. Android origins may pin `sha256_fingerprints` / `sha1_fingerprints`.
- Lookup — `POST /api-keys/lookup` with `{ "api_key": "fh_live_..." }` returns that key's project,
  safe representation, and origins (or 404). This is what lets an AI coding agent read a local
  project's `.env`, learn which FilesHub project it belongs to, and enforce origin restrictions.
- Supabase project vault — `GET /supabase-projects` (paginated, `?q=`/`?active=`),
  `GET /supabase-projects/{idOrRef}`, and `POST /supabase-projects/{idOrRef}/reveal`. Read and reveal a
  registered Supabase project's full credential set — API keys, JWT secret, Postgres connection details,
  S3 keys — plus ready-to-paste React (`VITE_*`), Node, and Laravel (`DB_*`) `.env` blocks. Read-only
  (projects are created/edited in the FilesHub admin), gated by the `can_manage_supabase` scope (a token
  without it gets 403 `TOKEN_PERMISSION_DENIED`, not 404, since the scope is a property of the caller's
  token). `{idOrRef}` is the numeric id or the `<ref>` of a `<ref>.supabase.co` URL. Every reveal is
  recorded. Secret presence (`has` flags) is reported without decrypting; a stored secret that cannot be
  decrypted (e.g. under a rotated APP_KEY) comes back `null` rather than failing the call. Derived
  endpoints (rest/auth/storage/graphql/functions/realtime/dashboard) are computed from the project URL.

Full reference: https://fileshub-docs.zaions.com/management-api/overview, the Supabase vault at
https://fileshub-docs.zaions.com/management-api/supabase-projects, and the agent recipe at
https://fileshub-docs.zaions.com/management-api/agent-workflow.

## Beyond storage: developer utilities

FilesHub also exposes 40+ stateless utility endpoint groups under `/api/v1`, including: hashing and
verification, Base64/URL encoding, JSON/CSV/XML/YAML converters, UUID/ULID/NanoID generators,
QR codes, short URLs, OTP send/verify, an encryption vault, image resize/compress/crop/convert,
PDF generation, link previews, sitemap/feed generation, webhooks, uptime monitors, and scheduled
tasks. Each takes the same `X-API-Key`; write utilities need `write` plus the group's service scope
(all enabled by default). Summary: https://fileshub-docs.zaions.com/api/utilities-index.

## Honest limits

- Single-region, locally-stored backend — not a global CDN. Use a CDN for large global media.
- Default 10 MB upload cap (configurable per deployment).
- No published client SDK yet; integrate over plain HTTP in any language. A TypeScript SDK is
  archived until the API stabilises.

## Author

Built and maintained by Ahsan Mahmood — https://aoneahsan.com ·
https://github.com/aoneahsan · https://www.npmjs.com/~aoneahsan
